notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. RCA at deliver/rca.md is the authoritative specification — user-reviewed and approved 2026-04-25 in Phase 2 before roadmap creation.

Two-step Outside-In TDD: Step 01-01 writes failing tests at the trait, store, and conformance levels (RED — committed with --no-verify per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds). Step 01-02 lands the LWW guard end-to-end: promote `dominates` to core, expose a trait-generic conformance harness behind a `test-utils` feature, replace sim's local `lww_dominates`, add the LWW-guarded write to `LocalObservationStore`, suppress emit on loss, and wire the conformance harness from both adapter test suites — all in a single cohesive commit. ADR-0012's third revision is paired with 01-02 via architect dispatch (per user-memory `feedback_delegate_to_architect`), not a separate roadmap step.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — sim's local `lww_dominates` is DELETED in the same commit as core's `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` lands; sim's `dominates_for_merge` (which preserves the `canary-bug`-feature flip) keeps its shape but calls `a.dominates(b)` on the new method. No `#[deprecated]` shim, no parallel comparator, no two-phase trait migration.

Trait conformance harness placement: `overdrive-core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance<T: ObservationStore + ?Sized>(store: &T)` exposed behind a NEW `test-utils` feature on `overdrive-core`. The harness is pure-Rust async (no real I/O of its own) and works against any `ObservationStore` impl. Both adapter crates pull `overdrive-core/test-utils` into their dev-deps. Store-local invokes it from `tests/integration/lww_conformance.rs` (gated `integration-tests` because the adapter touches real redb files). Sim invokes it from `tests/acceptance/lww_conformance.rs` (default lane — sim is in-memory, no real infra).

dst-lint compliance: `overdrive-core` is `crate_class = "core"` and dst-lint scans `src/**`. `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` is pure `Ord` over `(u64, NodeId)` — no `Instant`, no `tokio::net`, no `rand`, no `std::time`. The `testing` module is gated `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-utils"))]`; even when reachable it relies only on the `ObservationStore` trait surface and standard async — no banned APIs. The `xtask dst-lint` gate continues to pass.

Newtype completeness / typed-error / make-invalid-states-unrepresentable per .claude/rules/development.md: `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` returns `bool` — equal timestamps are NOT dominating (preserves the existing LWW idempotency case from `lww_dominates`); the tiebreak uses `writer` `Display` ordering exactly as the existing free function. No new error variants needed; the LWW path neither errors nor returns a typed result — losing writes succeed silently per the trait contract being added in the same commit.

Subscription emission semantics: when LWW rejects an incoming row, BOTH the redb `table.insert` AND the post-commit `self.emit(row)` are skipped. Subscribers must never observe a row the store will then refuse to return on read. This matches `SimObservationStore::apply_alloc_status` semantics where the fan-out send happens only inside the dominate branch (see crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs:170-176).

Existing acceptance case `overwrite_same_key_replaces_first_row` (counters 1→2 monotonic) keeps passing under the new LWW guard since `2.dominates(&1) == true`. The two new cases `out_of_order_alloc_status_does_not_regress` and `out_of_order_node_health_does_not_regress` write counter=5 first then counter=2 second and assert (a) the read after both writes returns the counter=5 row, AND (b) a subscriber attached before the second write does NOT receive the counter=2 row. These are the regression-guard tests for the actual bug.

Mutation testing scope (post-GREEN): per .claude/rules/testing.md §Mutation testing, run `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` foreground=false (mutation testing is the explicit exception per testing.md). Kill-rate gate ≥80%. The conformance harness's proptest cases MUST kill: the counter `>` vs `>=` flip in `dominates`, the `Less`/`Greater` arm swap, and the `writer.to_string() > rhs` vs `<` tiebreak inversion. If any of these survives, add an explicit case to the conformance harness that disambiguates it.

DST scope: SimObservationStore is unchanged in observable behaviour — it already had LWW; the only change is that its comparator now lives in core. All existing DST tests (`sim_observation_lww_converges.rs`, `sim_observation_gossip_mechanics.rs`, etc.) continue to pass without modification. The harness-determinism property is preserved because `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` is a pure function with no hidden state. No new DST scenario is needed; the regression surface is closed by the trait-generic conformance harness exercised against both adapters under their existing test-runner shapes.

Storage layout choice: read-then-conditional-insert inside the existing `begin_write` redb transaction. The crafter MUST decode only the prior row's `updated_at` field — either by decoding the full prior row via rkyv (acceptable; μs-scale per the RCA risk note) OR by introducing a small `LwwHeader` rkyv slice that fronts the full row's archive (more efficient but adds schema; not warranted for Phase 1). Default to the simple full-decode path; document the choice inline in `observation_backend.rs` near the new branch.

ADR-0012 third revision (paired with 01-02): user-memory `feedback_delegate_to_architect` is mandatory — the ADR edit MUST be dispatched to `@nw-solution-architect` (the architect who last touched ADR-0012, per `git log -- docs/product/architecture/adr-0012-observation-store-server-impl.md`). The architect strikes the §"No CRDT machinery, by design" subsection's claim that LWW is unnecessary, codifies the new trait contract (write applies LWW; losers do not mutate state and do not emit), and keeps the §"Restart semantics" rationale (Phase 1 single writer makes the guard trivially-satisfied today; the structural protection is for Phase 2). The architect also adds an `INSPECTED-DURING-FIX-LWW-MERGE` note to the revision header per the existing ADR revision pattern.

reviews.scope: RCA + proposed fix direction (promote `dominates` to core, suppress emit on LWW reject, trait-generic conformance suite under `integration-tests`/`acceptance` in both adapters, ADR-0012 third revision via architect)
reviews.notes: User confirmed: (1) root cause match across all three composing causes (trait under-specification, test coverage shape, comparator in wrong crate); (2) fix direction approved including conformance suite placement under `integration-tests` in store-local and `acceptance` in sim, plus single-cut deletion of sim's local `lww_dominates`; (3) no scope-narrowing — both AllocStatus AND NodeHealth row variants in this PR.

phases.name: Make ObservationStore::write LWW-guarded across both adapters
phases.description: Two-step Outside-In TDD bugfix. Step 01-01 writes the regression tests across three layers (RED — committed with --no-verify): (a) two new acceptance cases on store-local pinning that an older AllocStatusRow / NodeHealthRow arriving after a newer one does NOT regress the stored value AND does NOT fan out to subscribers; (b) a new integration test on store-local that invokes `overdrive_core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance` against a tempfile-backed `LocalObservationStore` (fails to compile because the testing module does not yet exist); (c) the same harness call from a new acceptance test on sim against `SimObservationStore::single_peer` (also fails to compile). Step 01-02 lands the cohesive single-cut fix: promote `lww_dominates` to `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` in core; add `test-utils` feature exposing `testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance`; replace sim's free-function call with `a.dominates(b)` and DELETE the sim's local `lww_dominates`; add the LWW-guarded read-then-conditional-insert to `LocalObservationStore::write` with suppressed emit on loss; update trait docstring on `ObservationStore::write`; both adapters' RED tests flip GREEN; full suite passes; mutation gate met on `dominates`. ADR-0012 third revision is paired with 01-02 via architect dispatch — not a separate roadmap step.

steps.name: RED regression tests — out-of-order writes + trait-generic conformance harness invocations
steps.description: Write four failing test artefacts that pin the LWW contract on `ObservationStore::write` across both adapters and across both row variants. (a) `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/local_observation_store.rs` — append two new `#[tokio::test]` functions: `out_of_order_alloc_status_does_not_regress` and `out_of_order_node_health_does_not_regress`. Each writes counter=5 first, then counter=2 (older), with all OTHER fields differing so a regression is observable; both call `subscribe_all` BEFORE the second write and assert the subscriber never receives the counter=2 row (use a short `tokio::time::timeout` on `next_some()` — the broadcast must be silent for the loser); after both writes call `alloc_status_rows()` / `node_health_rows()` and assert the returned row is the counter=5 row, NOT the counter=2 row. Both tests fail at runtime against current HEAD because `LocalObservationStore::write` performs a plain `table.insert` and emits on every successful commit. (b) NEW: `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/lww_conformance.rs` — calls `overdrive_core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance(&store).await` against a tempfile-backed `LocalObservationStore`. The body is a single test function `local_observation_store_satisfies_lww_conformance` and a one-line invocation. This will FAIL TO COMPILE against current HEAD because (i) `overdrive-core` does not yet expose a `testing` module, (ii) `overdrive-core/test-utils` is not yet a dev-dep on `overdrive-store-local`. (c) NEW: `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/lww_conformance.rs` — same shape but instantiated against `SimObservationStore::single_peer` (the existing constructor used by `sim_observation_lww_converges.rs`); same compile failure mode. (d) Wire all three new modules into the existing test entrypoints: append `mod lww_conformance;` inside the existing `mod integration { ... }` block in `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration.rs`, and append `mod lww_conformance;` inside the existing `mod acceptance { ... }` block in `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance.rs`. The two new acceptance cases on `local_observation_store.rs` need NO entrypoint wiring — they are appended to a file that is already a registered module. Implementation contract: the workspace is intentionally RED at the end of this commit (compile error in store-local integration target and sim acceptance target; runtime failure on the two store-local acceptance cases). Verify RED with `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` (compile error referencing missing `overdrive_core::testing`), `cargo check -p overdrive-sim --tests` (same compile error), and `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local -E 'test(out_of_order)'` (runtime assertion failures on the two new acceptance cases). The commit MUST be made with `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds; the commit body MUST explicitly cite that rule and name Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart. Commit trailer: `Step-ID: 01-01`.
steps.criteria: Four test additions: (1) two new `#[tokio::test]` functions appended to `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/acceptance/local_observation_store.rs` — `out_of_order_alloc_status_does_not_regress` and `out_of_order_node_health_does_not_regress`; (2) NEW file `crates/overdrive-store-local/tests/integration/lww_conformance.rs` invoking `overdrive_core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance`; (3) NEW file `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/lww_conformance.rs` invoking the same harness; (4) module declarations added inside the existing `mod integration { ... }` / `mod acceptance { ... }` blocks of the relevant `tests/integration.rs` / `tests/acceptance.rs` entrypoints. RED verification: `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` errors out on `unresolved import overdrive_core::testing` (NOT on a typo); `cargo check -p overdrive-sim --tests` errors out the same way; `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local -E 'test(out_of_order)'` exits non-zero with assertion failures showing the older row was returned by `alloc_status_rows()` / `node_health_rows()`. Commit message: `test(observation-store): pin LWW contract across LocalObservationStore + SimObservationStore (RED scaffold — see Step-ID: 01-01)` with `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; commit uses `git commit --no-verify` and the body explicitly cites .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Cause A (trait under-specification) — the new conformance harness invocation in both adapters' tests is the artefact that pins the trait contract; the harness's existence as a single shared function exercised against both adapters closes the structural absence of production from the convergence surface called out in RCA §Cause B / WHY 5B.

RCA §Cause B (test coverage shape) — the two new acceptance cases on store-local exercise out-of-order delivery directly (counter=5 then counter=2, assert no regression and no subscriber emission for the loser), which the existing `overwrite_same_key_replaces_first_row` (counters 1→2 monotonic) cannot distinguish from blind overwrite.

RCA §Cause C (comparator in wrong crate) — the conformance test files are positioned to be the call sites that prove core's new `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` is the single source of truth used by both adapters; the RED state where `overdrive_core::testing` does not yet exist is what 01-02 GREEN closes.
Both row variants are covered (AllocStatus + NodeHealth) per scope guardrail — partial closure leaves the same gap on `node_health`.

Tests live behind the appropriate gates per .claude/rules/testing.md §Integration vs unit gating: store-local integration test under `tests/integration/` (gated `integration-tests`); sim acceptance test under `tests/acceptance/` (default lane — sim is in-memory).

RED verified at the compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` and `cargo check -p overdrive-sim --tests` both error with `unresolved import overdrive_core::testing` (the proof that the harness does not yet exist).

RED verified at the runtime layer: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests -E 'test(out_of_order)'` exits non-zero showing the regression — the older row was retained.

Commit uses `git commit --no-verify` per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds — commit body explicitly references the rule and calls out the intentionally non-compiling state, naming Step 01-02 as the GREEN counterpart.
Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer.
steps.tier: Tier 1 (acceptance / integration — store-local integration test requires `integration-tests` feature; sim acceptance test runs in default lane).
quality_gates: RED verified at the compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests --tests` errors on `unresolved import overdrive_core::testing` (NOT on a typo or unrelated import).
RED verified at the compile layer: `cargo check -p overdrive-sim --tests` errors on the same `unresolved import`.

RED verified at the runtime layer: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-store-local --features integration-tests -E 'test(out_of_order)'` exits non-zero with a real LWW regression — the older row dominated.

Both new test files live behind the appropriate gates per testing.md §Integration vs unit gating: store-local integration test under `tests/integration/` (feature-gated); sim acceptance test under `tests/acceptance/` (default lane). No new feature flags introduced in this step.

No new env-var / cwd / process-global mutation introduced — `#[serial_test::serial(env)]` is NOT needed; the new tests use `tempfile::TempDir` (per-test) for the redb file and `tokio::sync::broadcast` for subscribers, both of which are isolation-clean.
Commit message includes `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer and explicit `--no-verify` rationale citing .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds.
deliverables: Module declarations added to existing `tests/integration.rs` / `tests/acceptance.rs` entrypoints (inline `mod integration { ... }` / `mod acceptance { ... }` block).
steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix; verify the existing test entrypoint shapes (store-local: `tests/integration.rs` uses inline `mod integration { ... }` block per testing.md §Integration vs unit gating; store-local: `tests/acceptance.rs` uses inline `mod acceptance { ... }`; sim: `tests/acceptance.rs` uses inline `mod acceptance { ... }`); confirm `SimObservationStore::single_peer` is the existing constructor used by `sim_observation_lww_converges.rs:44-46` (it is — keep symmetry). RED_ACCEPTANCE — write the two new out-of-order acceptance cases first; they are the human-readable proof of the user-facing regression. Each case follows the existing `overwrite_same_key_replaces_first_row` shape verbatim except the second write uses a STRICTLY OLDER timestamp (counter=2 after counter=5) AND the subscriber is attached BEFORE the second write so the test can assert the subscriber is silent for the loser. The subscriber assertion uses `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_millis(50), stream.next())` and asserts the result is `Err(_)` (timed out — no row arrived). For the alloc-status case the differing field is `state` or `svid_hash`; for the node-health case the differing field is `last_heartbeat` or `capacity`. RED_UNIT — write the two new `lww_conformance.rs` files: each is a single `#[tokio::test]` async fn that constructs an instance of the adapter and calls `overdrive_core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance(&store).await`. The store-local file constructs `LocalObservationStore::open(tmp.path().join("obs.redb"))?`; the sim file constructs `SimObservationStore::single_peer(seed)` (mirror `sim_observation_lww_converges.rs:44-46` exactly). The bodies do nothing else — the harness contains all the assertions. The compile failure on `unresolved import overdrive_core::testing` is the proof that the harness does not exist. GREEN — SKIPPED in this step (occurs in 01-02). COMMIT — `git commit --no-verify` with body: `RED scaffold per .claude/rules/testing.md §RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits. The store-local integration test and the sim acceptance test both reference `overdrive_core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance`, which lands in Step 01-02 along with the LWW-guarded production write. The store-local out-of-order acceptance cases fail at runtime because `LocalObservationStore::write` performs a plain `table.insert` and emits on every successful commit — also closed by Step 01-02. Step-ID: 01-01`. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` — do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly. Use the `tempfile::TempDir` pattern from existing acceptance tests for the redb path; use the `BroadcastStream` futures shape from `local_observation_store.rs` for subscriber assertions.

steps.name: GREEN cohesive fix — promote `dominates` to core + LWW-guarded write + test-utils harness + ADR-0012 revision
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Approved fix in a single cohesive commit (production code + test-utils harness + sim refactor + adapter dev-dep updates). ADR-0012 third revision is paired via architect dispatch (separate sub-step within this commit, dispatched as @nw-solution-architect — see implementation_notes). Production changes: (1) `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` — add `impl LogicalTimestamp { pub fn dominates(&self, other: &Self) -> bool }` mirroring the existing sim free function `lww_dominates` exactly: `(counter, writer)` lex order with writer `Display`-string tiebreak; equal timestamps return `false` (preserves the LWW idempotency case). Update the `ObservationStore::write` trait method docstring to specify the LWW contract: 'An incoming row whose `updated_at` does not dominate the existing row at the same primary key MUST NOT mutate state and MUST NOT be emitted on subscriptions. Adapters MUST consult `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` for this comparison.' (2) `crates/overdrive-core/Cargo.toml` — add new feature `test-utils = []` under `[features]`. (3) `crates/overdrive-core/src/lib.rs` — add `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-utils"))] pub mod testing;`. (4) NEW: `crates/overdrive-core/src/testing/mod.rs` — `pub mod observation_store;` and a one-line module-level docstring. (5) NEW: `crates/overdrive-core/src/testing/observation_store.rs` — `pub async fn run_lww_conformance<T: ObservationStore + ?Sized>(store: &T)` exercising the full LWW property surface. The harness MUST cover: (i) newer row dominates older row (counter strictly greater) — write older first then newer; assert read returns newer; (ii) older row arriving after newer row is rejected — write newer first then older; assert read still returns newer; (iii) equal-timestamp idempotency — re-deliver the same row; assert read is unchanged; (iv) tiebreak on writer when counters tie — same counter, two writers; assert higher-`Display` writer wins; (v) subscriber semantics — losers MUST NOT be emitted on the broadcast stream; the harness attaches a subscriber before the loser write and asserts a `tokio::time::timeout` of 50ms expires without delivery; (vi) both row variants exercised — every property tested for AllocStatusRow AND NodeHealthRow. The harness uses a small proptest section gated by `#[cfg(test)]`-equivalent — except since the harness IS test code, the proptest is unconditional. The proptest generates `(counter_a, counter_b, writer_a, writer_b)` tuples and asserts the harness's own merge order matches `LogicalTimestamp::dominates`'s order. (6) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs` — DELETE the local `fn lww_dominates(...)` (lines 229-238); update `dominates_for_merge` to call `a.dominates(b)` instead of `lww_dominates(a, b)` (preserving the `canary-bug` feature flip exactly); update doc comments referencing `lww_dominates` to reference `LogicalTimestamp::dominates`. (7) `crates/overdrive-store-local/src/observation_backend.rs::write` — replace the plain `table.insert` with read-then-conditional-insert inside the existing `begin_write` transaction: open the table; `table.get(key)?` for the prior bytes; if present, deserialise via rkyv `AlignedVec` (mirror the read path at lines 169-217 exactly) and extract `prior.updated_at`; compute `accepted = prior.is_none() || incoming.updated_at.dominates(&prior_unwrapped.updated_at)`; if accepted, `table.insert(key, bytes)`; if rejected, do nothing (table.insert is skipped); commit the txn unconditionally (the read alone does not write anything; rejected branches still commit a no-op transaction so the redb txn lifecycle remains balanced); return `accepted` from the spawn_blocking closure. After `.await??` resolves, only call `self.emit(row)` when `accepted == true`. Apply identically for AllocStatus and NodeHealth row variants. Add an inline `// NOTE:` comment near the new branch explaining the storage choice (decode full prior row vs `LwwHeader` slice — defaults to full decode per the roadmap note). (8) `crates/overdrive-store-local/Cargo.toml` — add `overdrive-core = { workspace = true, features = ["test-utils"] }` to `[dev-dependencies]`. (9) `crates/overdrive-sim/Cargo.toml` — same. (10) Verify Step 01-01's four new tests flip RED → GREEN within this commit. (11) `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` AND `cargo test --doc --workspace` AND `cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` — all green. (12) ADR-0012 third revision via architect dispatch (paired with this commit, NOT a separate roadmap step). The crafter dispatches `@nw-solution-architect` with the following scope: 'Revise `docs/product/architecture/adr-0012-observation-store-server-impl.md`. Strike the §"No CRDT machinery, by design" subsection's claim that LWW is unnecessary; codify the new trait contract: ObservationStore::write applies LWW under `LogicalTimestamp::dominates`; losers MUST NOT mutate state and MUST NOT emit on subscriptions. Keep §"Restart semantics" rationale (Phase 1 single writer makes the guard trivially-satisfied; the structural protection is for Phase 2). Add a third revision header note linking to deliver/rca.md.' Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: no compatibility shim; the sim's `lww_dominates` is DELETED in this same commit; both adapters reference `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` from this commit forward. Commit message: `fix(observation-store): apply LWW guard to LocalObservationStore::write` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production changes (single cohesive commit): (1) `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` lands in `overdrive-core` traits; trait docstring on `ObservationStore::write` documents LWW contract. (2) `overdrive-core/test-utils` feature added; `pub mod testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance<T>` exposed behind it. (3) Sim's local `lww_dominates` DELETED; sim's `dominates_for_merge` calls `a.dominates(b)` and preserves `canary-bug` flip. (4) `LocalObservationStore::write` performs read-then-conditional-insert inside the existing `begin_write` txn; LWW-rejected rows are NOT inserted and NOT emitted on subscriptions; both row variants handled identically. (5) `overdrive-store-local/Cargo.toml` and `overdrive-sim/Cargo.toml` add `overdrive-core/test-utils` to dev-dependencies. (6) ADR-0012 third revision lands via architect dispatch in this commit. Test verification: (7) Step 01-01's four tests flip RED → GREEN within this commit (compile error on `overdrive_core::testing` is closed; runtime regression on the two acceptance cases is closed). (8) All pre-existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` exits 0. (9) `cargo xtask dst-lint` continues to pass — `overdrive-core` additions are pure (no `Instant`, no `tokio::net`, no `rand`). (10) Mutation gate (post-GREEN): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` (run with `run_in_background: true` per testing.md §Mutation testing exception); kill-rate ≥80% on the diff; no surviving mutations on the `>` / `>=` flip in `dominates`, the `Less`/`Greater` swap, or the tiebreak `>` / `<` inversion. Single-cut migration: no `lww_dominates` call sites remain in `overdrive-sim` (a `git grep 'lww_dominates' crates/overdrive-sim` returns no matches); no `#[deprecated]` aliases. Commit message: `fix(observation-store): apply LWW guard to LocalObservationStore::write` with `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`). Post-GREEN smoke check: `git diff --name-only` shows the full set of production and test files modified; the diff includes the trait change, the test-utils harness, the sim refactor, the LocalObservationStore guard, the dev-dep updates, AND the architect-revised ADR-0012 — all in one commit.
acceptance_criteria: RCA §Cause A (trait under-specification — closure) — `ObservationStore::write` trait docstring now specifies LWW contract; `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` exists on the core type as the single comparator definition; both adapters reference it.

RCA §Cause B (test coverage shape — closure) — `overdrive-core::testing::observation_store::run_lww_conformance<T>` is invoked from BOTH adapter test suites with their respective adapter instances; the structural absence of production from the convergence surface is closed.

RCA §Cause C (comparator in wrong crate — closure) — `lww_dominates` no longer exists in `overdrive-sim`; the comparator lives in `overdrive-core` as `LogicalTimestamp::dominates`; both adapters call it from the single source of truth.
Step 01-01's four tests flip RED → GREEN within this commit without modification (proving the fix is load-bearing and the tests pin the invariant).
Subscription emission semantics: losers are NOT fanned out on `subscribe_all` — verified by the new acceptance cases AND by the conformance harness's subscriber-silence proptest.
Both row variants covered (AllocStatus AND NodeHealth) — partial closure does not pass the gate.
All pre-existing tests remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` exits 0; `cargo test --doc --workspace` exits 0.
Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: sim's `lww_dominates` deleted in same commit; no compatibility shim; no parallel comparator.

`cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` green on the touched crates; typed errors per .claude/rules/development.md (no new `eyre::Report` on library boundaries; no new `.expect()` outside binary entrypoints).
`cargo xtask dst-lint` green — `overdrive-core` additions stay banned-API-clean.

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE — re-read RCA §Approved fix; verify the existing sim adapter's `dominates_for_merge` shape (it wraps `lww_dominates` with the `canary-bug` flip — keep that wrapper, only change its delegate to `a.dominates(b)`); verify no other `lww_dominates` call sites exist outside `crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs` (a `git grep 'lww_dominates' crates/` sweep — comment-only references in tests/docstrings can stay as they are descriptive; production references must be updated). RED_ACCEPTANCE — Step 01-01 already wrote the outermost RED tests; verify they currently fail (compile error on `overdrive_core::testing`; runtime regression on the two new acceptance cases). RED_UNIT — for any internal helper introduced inside `LocalObservationStore::write` (e.g. an `extract_prior_alloc_status_updated_at` helper or a generic `lww_check<R>` over both row variants), write a same-crate unit test that pins the helper's behaviour BEFORE writing it. Otherwise no new internal helpers — the in-line read-then-conditional-insert is short enough not to warrant abstraction (per development.md "Don't add features, refactor, or introduce abstractions beyond what the task requires"). GREEN — implement in this order: (1) `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` (the comparator must exist before any caller can use it); (2) `pub mod testing` + `run_lww_conformance` (the harness depends on `dominates`); (3) sim's `dominates_for_merge` delegate swap + DELETE local `lww_dominates`; (4) sim's Cargo.toml dev-dep; verify sim acceptance test now compiles; (5) store-local's LWW guard in `write`; (6) store-local's Cargo.toml dev-dep; verify store-local integration test compiles. Verify the bump-and-capture inside the redb spawn_blocking closure: the `accepted` boolean must be derived INSIDE the closure (where redb begin_write is held) and returned out — `self.emit(row)` happens AFTER the closure resolves, gated on `accepted == true`. Run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` at the end of GREEN to confirm zero regressions across the workspace; this is the input to Phase 3.5 post-merge integration gate. ARCHITECT-DISPATCH (for ADR-0012 third revision): per user-memory `feedback_delegate_to_architect`, the ADR edit is NOT made inline. The crafter MUST dispatch `@nw-solution-architect` (the architect that last touched ADR-0012, per `git log -- docs/product/architecture/adr-0012-observation-store-server-impl.md`) with the following self-contained brief: 'Revise `docs/product/architecture/adr-0012-observation-store-server-impl.md` (third revision). Read deliver/rca.md at docs/feature/fix-observation-lww-merge/deliver/rca.md for context. Strike the §"No CRDT machinery, by design" subsection's claim that LWW is unnecessary; codify the new trait contract: `ObservationStore::write` applies LWW under `LogicalTimestamp::dominates`; losers MUST NOT mutate state and MUST NOT emit on subscriptions. Keep §"Restart semantics" rationale unchanged (Phase 1 single writer makes the guard trivially-satisfied; the structural protection is for Phase 2). Add a third revision header note (mirror the existing `Revised 2026-04-24` header pattern). Reference the new comparator location: `LogicalTimestamp::dominates` in `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs`. Stage the change but do NOT commit — the crafter will include it in the cohesive 01-02 commit.' COMMIT — `git commit` (no --no-verify); the commit body explicitly states the trait change, the test-utils harness, the sim refactor, the LocalObservationStore guard, AND the architect-revised ADR-0012 — single cohesive commit. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`. Post-commit smoke check: run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` AND `cargo test --doc --workspace` AND `cargo clippy --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` one more time to confirm zero regressions across the full workspace; this is the input to Phase 3.5 post-merge integration gate. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per testing.md §Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`. Invoke `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs`; wait for completion via the shell ID; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate; on any surviving mutation, add an explicit conformance-harness case that disambiguates it AND re-run mutation. Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass to restore any mutated source.
